summary: EXP-349 closes ISS-1959 (WIN-014.c7 H3, moderate, queue_id=329) with a single find_replace patch on data/wins.json:WIN-014.detail_evidence. The patch replaces the loose existing sentence 'The 2009 eclipse traversed China at coverage approaching 100% along the path.' with precise totality-corridor geography (Chongqing–Wuhan–Hangzhou–Shanghai, ~258 km path width) and forecloses the off-path partial-coverage defender pivot via two structural rebuttals: (a) the dome publishes no per-site coverage threshold for κ — κ=1.677 nT/µGal is a constant, not a coverage-gated function — so any coverage-based exemption is a post-hoc scope condition; (b) at 70% partial coverage the κ-arithmetic still predicts 2.1–6.3 µGal at the off-totality SG sites, 2× to 60× above the 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction floor. Kernel-of-truth (non-uniform site coverage) is acknowledged head-on. Verdict (Refuted by Data) preserved. Recommended action: PATCH; mark ISS-1959 fixed-pending-verification. review_class=deep-attack per tracker (overrode class_hint=verification on source ISS via PROP-034 hybrid rule). Anchor uniqueness verified (count=1 in data/wins.json). No conflict with EXP-384 (different anchor in same detail_evidence), EXP-358 (citation cluster — different content), or EXP-364 (SEC-6.14, different file).
